HGSS.457.0000:
Welcome!\f
Just to let you know, all the large
specimens have been taken to the\r
other museums for display.\f
So for now, admission is free
of charge!\f
Please go ahead.
HGSS.457.0001:
Did you know that you can
extract Pokémon from Fossils?\f
If you ever need to, let me take care
of it for you!\f
HGSS.457.0002:
Please come by again if you ever want to
extract Pokémon.
HGSS.457.0003:
I’ll extract it for you.\f
Come back after a while!
HGSS.457.0004:
Hi, there!\f
I have extracted a Pokémon from
your Fossil!\f
It is called [0100,0000,0000].
Take good care of it!\f
HGSS.457.0005:
Oops!
Your party seems full.\f
Why don’t you go to the PC
to make room for it?
HGSS.457.0006:
Hey!
You don’t have any Fossils.\f
I heard that sometimes you find Fossils
when you smash rocks at the\r
Ruins of Alph and other places.
HGSS.457.0007:
Do you want to extract a Pokémon
from the [0108,0000,0000]? [0200,0000]
HGSS.457.0009:
Would you like to give a nickname
to the [0100,0001,0000] you received? [0200,0000]
HGSS.457.0010:
They say that meteorites struck
somewhere near Pewter City.\f
We would love to display them here
in our museum, but...\f
These meteorites are said to
emit strange energy and cannot\r
be carried away...
HGSS.457.0011:
There are two places that I love!
One is the Ruins of Alph in Johto...\f
The other is the Pewter Museum!
It’s so quiet and makes me feel calm.\f
Do you feel its admirable history
silently creeping up on you?
HGSS.457.0012:
I should be grateful for my long life.\f
Never did I think I would get to
see the bones of a dragon!
HGSS.457.0013:
..................\f
All those shining stars at night are
the stones I’ll never get to know...
HGSS.457.0014:
Steven: People who dedicate themselves
to what they believe stand out in\r
a crowd.\f
But, my passion is no less than yours.\f
HGSS.457.0016:
This stone is...so mysterious!\f
Something seems to be shining inside...
The stone is modest...yet hiding\r
something wonderful!\f
You should have it studied
by experts.\f
HGSS.457.0017:
[0103,0000,0000] handed the Enigma Stone
to the researcher!\f
HGSS.457.0018:
I will check it.
Can you hold on a second?\f
HGSS.457.0019:
Thank you for your patience!
It took me a little while,\r
but I have confirmed it!\f
This is an Orb that contains
what is called [FF00,0002]Soul Dew[FF00,0000].\f
HGSS.457.0020:
Soul Dew is no ordinary thing
and is rarely seen.\r
I would love to study it closely.
HGSS.457.0021:
Steven: Soul Dew is said to be the
solidified spirits of LATIAS and LATIOS.\f
It has the power to bring out the
true potential of LATIAS and LATIOS.\f
The spirits of these Pokémon and
Soul Dew are said to call out to\r
each other...\f
The only thing that puzzles me is
the place Soul Dew was found.\r
Why Kanto?\f
HGSS.457.0022:
Stringy and winding...
Is this a plant Fossil?
HGSS.457.0023:
It’s a curving, sharp Fossil.
This may be...\r
the Fossil of a carnivore?
HGSS.457.0024:
It’s flat and looks hard.
This may be...\r
a part that protected its body?
HGSS.457.0025:
Solid amber tree sap has
something inside!\f
If you can take it out, you can
extract a Pokémon from\r
ancient times...?
HGSS.457.0026:
Small bones are put together to form
some kind of a Pokémon!
HGSS.457.0027:
The rough stone has words etched
into it...\f
“This is an unidentified object
found at Mt. Moon. It is possibly a\r
moon rock.”
HGSS.457.0028:
There are thick and well-read books!\f
“The Principles of Fossil Extraction”\r
“Archaeology Manual”\r
“Lost Pokémon”\r
“Pokémon Ancestors”\r
“Friendships of Trainers and Pokémon”\r
“Caring at the Loamy Layer of Kanto”
HGSS.457.0029:
Our museum mainly displays
Fossils and specimens from Kanto.\f
Please enjoy the extinct Pokémon
through their remembrance, and\r
step through time to the ancient world.
